div1.entryFree: Revolution, change produced by time: that I might see what the old world could say to this composed wonder of your frame; whether we are mended, or whether better they, or whether r. be the same, Sonn. 59, 12 (whether change be identity, i. e. no change). see the r. of the times make mountains level, H4B III, 1, 46. here's fine r., an we had the trick to see't, Hml. V, 1, 98. the present pleasure, by r. lowering, does become the opposite of itself, Ant. I, 2, 129. Apparently == any change: full of forms, figures, shapes, objects, ideas, apprehensions, motions, --s, LLL IV, 2, 70 (Holofernes' speech).
